channel.title: Captain Dadders — Flight Expeditions
channel.description: Following the Lewis & Clark Corps of Discovery route by air — MSFS 2024 expedition blog.

item.title: Leg 7: Below the Falls — KJVY to KY8
item.description: Departing Clark Regional and heading downstream past Louisville and Clarksville — the same stretch the full Corps of Discovery first traveled together in October 1803 — past a remarkable corridor of power plants, around the Horseshoe Bend of the Ohio near Leavenworth, Indiana, and past the Cannelton Hydroelectric Plant before landing at Hancock County Airport / Lewis Field in Lewisport, Kentucky.

item.title: Leg 6: Clark's Country — 4KT4 to KJVY
item.description: A peaceful cruise down the Ohio past the Milton-Madison Bridge and the massive Clifty Creek power plant, landing at Clark Regional Airport in Clark County, Indiana — the county and airport bearing the name of William Clark's older brother George Rogers Clark, the Revolutionary War hero who secured the Northwest Territory and founded Clarksville just downstream.

item.title: Leg 5: Queen City to the Fossil Lick — KLUK to 4KT4
item.description: Flying past downtown Cincinnati — the Great American Ballpark, the Bengals' stadium, and the skyline — then following the Ohio River southwest into Kentucky, landing at Steele's Bottom near Warsaw, close to Big Bone Lick, where Lewis collected mastodon fossils for President Jefferson before departing on the expedition.

item.title: Leg 4: River Highway to Cincinnati — I41 to KLUK
item.description: Past West Virginia at last, with Kentucky on the left and Ohio on the right. The Ohio River in 1803 was a bustling highway of westward migration — settlers, merchants, traders, and Native peoples all sharing the corridor. Towns lined the banks just as they do today, and the river barges are still going strong.

item.title: Leg 3: Above the Morning Fog — 75D to I41
item.description: The river valley was draped in mist with hilltops emerging above the clouds — exactly the kind of scenery Lewis would have seen pushing downriver in the autumn of 1803.

item.title: Leg 2: Wheeling and the Mound — 1G8 to 75D
item.description: Following the Ohio River south from Toronto, Ohio through the industrial river towns of Steubenville and Wheeling, West Virginia — passing the remarkable Grave Creek Mound, the largest surviving Adena burial mound in the United States, before landing at New Martinsville.

item.title: Leg 1: Departing Pittsburgh — 31D to 1G8
item.description: Lifting off from Inter County Airport in Irwin, Pennsylvania and following Meriwether Lewis's exact 1803 route down the Ohio River — past the Point where three rivers meet, over the historic Weirton/Steubenville corridor, and landing at Toronto, Ohio.
